# The Privacy Act 1988 (Cth)

## Overview

The **Privacy Act 1988** (Cth) is the primary federal legislation governing the handling of personal information in Australia. It establishes the **Australian Privacy Principles** (APPs) which regulate the collection, use, disclosure, and storage of personal information.

## Key Provisions

| APP | Title | Requirement |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| APP 1 | Open and transparent management | Privacy policy required |
| APP 3 | Collection of solicited information | Must be necessary and lawful |
| APP 6 | Use or disclosure | Only for primary purpose |
| APP 11 | Security of personal information | Reasonable steps to protect |
| APP 13 | Correction of personal information | Must correct on request |

## Jurisdiction

The Privacy Act applies to:

1. Australian Government agencies
2. Organisations with annual turnover exceeding $3 million
3. Health service providers
4. Organisations that trade in personal information

## Key Cases

The landmark case of **Australian Information Commissioner v Facebook Inc** established that foreign corporations operating in Australia are subject to the Privacy Act. In **Privacy Commissioner v Telstra Corporation**, the court clarified the scope of APP 11 security obligations.

> The Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) represents one of the most comprehensive privacy regulation frameworks in the Asia-Pacific region, predating the GDPR by nearly three decades.
